title: Critical care safety-net documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-led monitoring, transfer and information in suspected sepsis.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.7 — emergency transfer, monitoring, and information

examination.text: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source_limitations: Diagnosis, physiology, supports, trajectory, thresholds, ceiling, contact and escalation actions are absent.
No deterioration threshold, treatment, transfer or resuscitation action is generated.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
